mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high real sales of new autos and low federal funds rates indicate strong consumer credit availability and robust demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative mean forward return or Rank IC < 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding corporate output and improving profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during late-cycle or recession regimes, or produces negative returns in >60% of early-cycle instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic activity and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of positive GDP growth quarters over a 10-year sample, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation rate exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of monetary tightening and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >70% of high-inflation months or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicate synchronized labor and construction sector expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative mean return or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in >60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high unemployment rates above 9 percent signal structural labor market weakness and suppressed consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn positive in >65% of high-unemployment months or Rank IC < 0.02 during the specified regime.
